question.text: A top-up health insurance plan is beneficial in retirement because:

answer.text: It replaces the need for a base health insurance policy
It provides additional coverage at a lower premium once the base policy limit is exhausted
It covers only outpatient expenses not covered by the base policy
It is available only to individuals below 60 years of age

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A top-up plan kicks in after the base policy's deductible (typically equal to the base sum insured) is exhausted. It provides additional coverage at significantly lower premiums compared to increasing the base policy. **The Trap:** Option A is wrong — a top-up requires a base policy. Option C confuses top-up with OPD coverage. Top-up covers hospitalization costs above the deductible. **Cross-Exam Context:** Health insurance structuring for retirement appears in Series XVII and connects to insurance concepts in Series X-B. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Recommend a combination: base health policy (Rs.5-10 lakh) + top-up/super top-up (Rs.25-50 lakh). This provides comprehensive coverage at an affordable total premium.
